name: Chicken with Orange Sauce- New Ama Cookbook
recipeIngredient: 1 cup onion slices (or one medium onion with ends removed and sliced)
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the chicken and pat it dry with paper towels. Cut into serving pieces and remove all visible fat.
2. Prepare a baking sheet by lightly spraying with vegetable oil cooking spray.
3. Sprinkle the chicken pieces with paprika and place them on the prepared baking sheet.
4. Broil the chicken, about 6 inches from the heat, until lightly browned on all sides (4-5 minutes). Remove from the broiler and transfer to a Dutch oven or large, deep skillet, adding onion slices.
5. In a small bowl, whisk together orange sauce ingredients: frozen orange juice concentrate, water, brown sugar, parsley, soy sauce, sherry, and ground ginger.
6. Pour the sauce over the chicken and onions in the pot.
- STOVE TOP: Bring to a boil over medium-high heat, then reduce to a simmer, cover, and continue cooking until the chicken is done and tender (about 55 minutes).
- ALTERNATIVE: Place browned pieces and onion slices in a casserole dish. Pour orange juice mixture over all and bake, covered, at 350°F for 55-60 minutes or until the chicken is tender.
Makes 4 servings; each serving contains approximately 273 calories (28% from fat), 9 grams fat (2 grams saturated), 14 grams carbohydrate, 34 grams protein, 132 mg sodium, 89 mg cholesterol, 37 mg calcium, and 1 gram fiber. Serve warm over egg-free noodles if desired, or chilled for an outdoor meal. Use any or all chicken parts; breasts may require less cooking time.
